The color and element of gold, mathematically/physically:  its nucleus lacks the outward facing ring/cylinder/horn singularities, relative to other elements.  This blunts the ring/cylinder/horn singularities in the electron shells.  Filamentous photons produced have similar blunted singularities.  By blunted, I mean like the throat of a bottle of soda, as compared to the more common hypodermic needle tip shape of other elements. Dark matter during a Big Bang has a blunted pair of singularities from impacts from surrounding black holes.  This corresponds to the cubic root of the imaginary number, the square root of 75 (which factors as 3 x 5 x 5), and to a golden color, although photons are not involved significantly during the initial stages of a Big Bang event. The color brown:  slowed down dark matter, on the verge of not rotating enough to remain stable as a particle.  This is the converse of gold.  Bromine might fit for having a nucleus that would only slightly blunt singularities and correspond to brown. Split-complex numbers relate to all this - the pot of gold at the end of the rainbow. Aligned singularities in protons (baryonic matter) determine the frequency/color that we eventually see through photons. In dark matter, it is paired up, alternating from the central plane, adjacent singularities determining frequency/color. If you look at all the elements, you only see variations of red, orange, or yellow, which lie near the rarefied ring/cylinder/horn singularity as mapped for thickness that can sustain ripples of energy throughout the particle, corresponding to the frequency/color.
Key Laws of Nature:      Newton's Laws of Motion:         First Law (Inertia): An object remains at rest or in uniform motion unless acted upon by a force.         Second Law (F=ma): The force acting on an object is equal to its mass times its acceleration.         Third Law (Action and Reaction): For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.      Law of Universal Gravitation:         Every mass attracts every other mass in the universe with a force proportional to their masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.      Conservation Laws:         Conservation of Energy: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed from one form to another.         Conservation of Mass: Mass cannot be created or destroyed in an isolated system.         Conservation of Momentum: The total momentum of a closed system remains constant if no external forces act on it.      Thermodynamics:         First Law: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred or converted (similar to the conservation of energy).         Second Law: The entropy of an isolated system always increases over time, leading to the irreversibility of natural processes.         Third Law: As temperature approaches absolute zero, the entropy of a system approaches a constant minimum.      Electromagnetic Laws:         Coulomb's Law: The force between two charged particles is proportional to the product of their charges and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them.         Maxwell's Equations: Describe how electric and magnetic fields propagate and interact.

Here's something to consider in regard to Einstein's motion based time dilation theory. If there are two circular train tracks, one with a diameter of 1 unit and the other with a diameter of 2 units, for example, and the train on the smaller circular track was moving at half the velocity of the train on the larger circular track, what would their relative velocity be? It would be zero. Well now Einstein has a problem, because the trains are moving at different absolute angular velocities, as could be determined from the centrifugal force experienced on each train, but time on both would have to be identical from the viewpoint of observers in each train because there is zero relative velocity between them. Time dilation has been nullified simply by the two trains having angular velocities such that the distance between them never changes.   This is why Einstein was wrong when he predicted that clocks on the equator would run slower than clocks near the poles, there is actually zero relative velocity between the two clocks, because the distance between them is constant, so why would their times be different? The explanation that Einstein supporters came up with to explain his glorious failure is far from credible, that gravitational time dilation just happens by pure coincidence to cancel out the supposed motion based time dilation. The odds of that working out perfectly are obviously infinitesimal and the real explanation is so obvious that why even go to an alternate one which is less likely than being struck by lightning at the same time as being attacked by a shark while swimming in a duck pond?
This subject triggers me so much. First I would like to point to a phenomenon everybody overlooks, and is not mentioned or represented in the animation in this video, which is that from your frame of reference the spaceship radially flattens as it approaches the event horizon. The integrated length from an external observer to the event horizon is infinite, which translates to an ever increasing length contraction of the spaceship. At the time of event horizon crossing, it must have 0 length. Sounds wrong? Well, that brings me to my second point: it is not an "illusion" that the objects are hovering frozen above the event horizon. They are actually there, stopped, in the infinite space between us and the event horizon, and will be there forever or until the BH evaporates (if Hawking radiation is real). The crossing never really happens. Eddington-Finkelstein coordinates don't matter if you are the external observer! In GR you can't just extrapolate what will happen in one frame of reference to another if the mapping between both world lines contains a singularity! You can't get rid of the event horizon singularity by just hiding it! The time of crossing in proper time maps to a time infinitely in the future for our coordinate time, which means no crossing has happened, and will not happen, ever, from our POV! Again, this is not an "illusion".  And of course, everything in the video after that is just fun math play with no physicality. In math you can of course compactify infinites and stretch points to lines, but that doesn't necessarily mean anything. The "it's just coordinates" argument goes both ways.

Time is not real, but we use this flow of events (as if in the direction of an arrow) to recognise that this flow of events is different to another observer than yourself...put more simply, the vents are squashed or stretched...depending on your position and speed relative to the other observer...for example...Let's say you look at your watch and the time now is 8pm...or 9pm (does not matter for this explanation) Just let's say 8pm and you have a spaceship to take you from where you are at 8pm and leave the earth and then travel in a big loop, so that you return to where you started. Ok, now you look at your watch and it shows just one hour has gone by. But that is just one hour as far as you are concerned, though if you travelled very fast, such as somewhere approaching the speed of light, then several hours may have past by the time you return and your doctor's appointment is now just a few minutes away instead of several hours away. The operative function here is that time is relative to the observer. Yes...I know it sounds contra-intuitive, but it has been proven by having two synchronised atomic clocks, one clock kept on the ground and one put on board a jet airplane for several hours. When the jet plane landed, the clocks were put side by side and the clock in the jet plane showed that the time was behind the clock on the ground...which means time had gone faster for the jet plane clock...so time effectively can be seen to have slowed down for those (and the clock) back on earth from your perspective as an observer...but though it sounds weird...this is a real phenomenon. Please don't get me started on black holes : )

I feel I must say something about this treatment of Einstein's math. The person who invented it made some *really* bad assumptions and have thus led people down the road of misinformation.  Let me see if I can illuminate you.¬†  You said that you have to go faster than light to get to the Einstein-Rosen bridge (it's not an Einstein-Rosen bridge, but I'll get to that), but if you can go faster than light, the Einstein-Rosen bridge becomes unnecessary. If you can go faster than light, then you can *just* escape the black hole. According to your chart, if you escape the black hole one way, you end up in our universe and if you escape a different way you end up in a parallel universe.  However, that's not the way it works. It you go faster than light to escape a black hole, you merely end up outside of the black hole. That means that the area on the left that is labeled "parallel universe" is actually *our* universe.  You can see this as true if you plot your graph in three dimensions. This is a graph of a light cone, so the cone shape reveals that the area on the left side of the graph is just a wrap-around part of this universe.¬†  Therefore, that point isn't an Einstein-Rosen bridge. It is merely the point of view of someone who is on the event horizon of a black hole. And that area at the bottom is also our universe, but it's just the visible part.  And that's not taking into account that the speed of light actually isn't a constant. It's close enough to fool us into thinking that it is, but it falls short enough for the Universe to avoid being silly. I wish there would be more effort on the study of physical black holes, and I say this from the mindset of a theoretical physicist. A big issue I always have with the standard narration is on the "from the outside, You can never see something falling inside". There is always some implicit implication that it is a mirage, a wrong-seeing, but the reality is that things have fallen in. But I disagree as we (from the outside) see like that because that is the causal relation that we maintain with everything that make up the black hole. And this point is essential, that all of it still "presents" itself to, i.e. causally affects, the rest of the outer universe. This is the physical black hole, physis is what is presented, what causes, thus it is physical to this "outer" universe what affects it. Otherwise we have a worse issue than the one which concerned Newton. Anything on and inside the event horizon cannot affect the outer universe, then what causes the spacetime curvature in the latter? How is the black hole presented as localized in spacetime? The mathematical continuation across the event horizon does not get away with the bifurcation of the causation flow. We need the causation effected from all that has not yet fallen and never will fall into the event horizon to have physical black holes within our ("outer") universe, it is not a mere matter of appearance or a misleading mirage. Physical black holes should be studied as the separation of liminal and residual physical quantities (respect to how they remain effective toward the outward universe) as the physical limit which is the event horizon is approached. This in relation to QM and decoherence.

I'm still not satisfied that anything can enter a black hole. As you get closer to the event horizon, it's not just that photons take longer to get to outside observers, time is moving slower for me. When I'm at the event horizon, an infinite amount of time needs to pass for an outside observer before I cross that event horizon. Assuming I do cross it, it'd have to happen AFTER whatever the death of the universe looks like (heat death, big rip, proton decay, whatever). In other words, even if something can enter a black hole, it should never happen while we're here, experiencing time.  The only remotely satisfying explanation I've ever heard for this is that black holes aren't static, and the event horizon can be "tugged" around by other gravitational masses. So as I get close to the event horizon, it sort of "rises up" to meet me. So it's less that I'm crossing the event horizon, and more that it's crossing me. If I'm "frozen" in time at one millimeter away from the event horizon, but the event horizon "moves" up to encompass me, the outside universe doesn't need to experience infinite time before I cross the boundary.   But then I start thinking about gravitational waves, and how it seems to be that gravity propagates at the speed of light. So if something enters the black hole, how does the event horizon "know" to expand accordingly? Gravity can't escape if it's propagating at the speed of light. In which case we're right back where we started, where the event horizon needs an infinite amount of time to cross me.   I dunno, this stuff breaks my brain. I don't get how black holes can exist at all. But they obviously do, so I'm not sure what I'm not understanding

24:30 - This is the point where physicists need to realize they've reached the limits of usefulness of their mathematical models; the point at which reality departs from their models. Physicists often suffer from an arrogance that implies their mathematical models actually define reality rather than imperfectly model it. They readily assume that their models tell us certain things about reality that the actually don't, merely because the math allows it. Any advanced mathematician ought to realize the difference between unbounded nonsense solutions (like dividing by zero) and useful solutions that correctly model reality. But physicists like too much playing in la-la land that they get excited over fake solutions as if they were real possibilities, like time running in reverse. They do this merely because their limited mathematical models seem to offer the possibility. That's when it's time for a reality check. And actually this video does a good job of elucidating how poorly chosen transformations or coordinate systems or simplifying assumptions can lead to nonsensical mathematical results. Rememeber: the math is just a model with limited descriptive properties, not the generator of reality itself. And the example he gives at 26:08 is a faulty analogy. The arbitrary assignment of negative latitude numbers to the southern hemisphere is much different than the la-la-land assignment of negative time values to describe the flow of time and space. You cannot learn anything about what is real by assigning arbitrary coordinate systems on things and then claiming "the msth says..." The coordinate systems must first accurately model reality before you can learn anything from them.
It seems to me (and I've heard before that this is an actual theory supposed by real physicists) that the straightforward solution is that technically speaking black holes *don't* exist, only arbitrarily close approximations to them do: as two particles approach each other's Schwarzchild radii, time for them slows down, and it takes a very long time (on the fast scale of particle interactions) for them to finish that close brush-by with each other, during which time any other particles that approach those particles' joint Schwarzchild radius experience the same time dilation, and so on, in an enormous "traffic jam" of particles approaching close by but never finishing crossing the line that would be an event horizon if a proper black hole could ever finish forming. Given a long enough time of no more particles joining the traffic jam, the ones furthest out would eventually finish their close brush-by with the rest and escape, which would lessen the total time dilation that the rest are experiencing and let some others eventually finish their brush-bys in slightly less time, and as more and more particles escape the remaining ones can escape faster and faster, just like is predicted from Hawking radiation.  So if you could somehow avoid spaghettification as you fell into a black hole, what you would see is time outside of you speed up (as your internal time slows down), and over unimaginable aeons unfolding in fast-motion before you, the black hole you're falling into would evaporate out from under you until such point that its "event horizon" is small enough that you're not on a course to collide with it anymore, but instead will just pass near it and slingshot back out into space.

@Veritasium, I have a problem with the idea that "if you could see that light, then in theory you would see everything that has ever fallen into the blackhole" [1:04]  To understand the problem, consider a photographer that takes several hundred pictures of different subjects; and then projects all the pictures onto the same screen at once.  In theory, on the screen you have a representation of every subject of the photographer's art, but in practise what you would see on the screen would be essentially a white screen with, perhaps, some effectively random darker shadings.  This would be a direct consequence of the different shadings of each individual picture not matching each other, so that light areas on some pictures overlap dark areas on others.  And because the dark areas represent only the absence of light, the end product at each point in the screen would be the additive sum of the brightness at that point of each picture.  This is almost directly analogous to the situation for light from objects crossing the event horizon.  The analogy only breaks down in that, the contrast in brightness between different points on an object falling into a black hole will decrease in direct proportion to the amount of the red shift.  At the point when we in fact cease to see the light, the contrast becomes zero (ie, there would be no difference in brightness between the brightest and darkest points on the object, if we could still see it).  The effect of both of these factors is to destroy the information content of light from the object falling into the black hole.  As the object falling into the black hole approaches the event horizon, we obtain less and less information about it until we obtain none.  But if we are obtaining no information, in what meaningful sense do we see it?

Seluruh bintang dan benda(planet2 dan zat partikel) semua itu akan mendingin dengan mengeluarkan suatu partikel gelap(dark partikel(suatu partikel tak kasat mata)     Partikel gelap mempunyai kecepatan yang sangat tinggi ketika berakselerasi bisa menembus atom2 yang ada dan sebagaimana akselerasi bom thermobarik, akibat kecepatan partikel gelap terkecil itu menyebabkan daya tarik ketika benda dengan kecepatan tak terduga berakselerasi.     sedangkan yang membuat antar planet atau antar bintang mempunyai daya tolak menolak ialah ketika partikel gelap menabrak partikel gelap lainnya dan partikel gelap cenderung menjauh dari inti atom yang di tinggalkan.    sementara partikel gelap yang berakselerasi akan menciptakan vacum, inilah yang menyebabkan adanya suatu gravitasi, semakin dekat, banyak dan kuat ledakan partikel gelap dari suatu benda. Maka akan menyebabkan akselerasi vacum partikel gelap semakin kuat(gravitasi)      Tanda tanda planet atau bintang yang mempunyai gravitasi kuat ialah semakin besar permukaan keras suatu bintang atau benda dan semakin panas(panas suatu benda dapat disimpulkan sebagai ketidakseimbangan partikel(yakni terjadi peluruhan atau kebocoran di dalam atom dan atom terus memuntah kan partikel gelap))suatu benda atau bintang tersebut maka semakin kuat gravitasi nya(gaya vacum nya)     Partikel gelap menyebabkan antargalaksi saling menjauh di karenakan partikel gelap akan dengan sendirinya karena proses pendinginan.  .
US scientists have claimed that a lesser-known Austrian physicist, Friedrich Hasenohrl, may have played a significant role in the development of EinsteinвАЩs famous equation, E=mc¬≤. In a forthcoming study in the European Physical Journal H, Stephen Boughn from Haverford College, Pennsylvania, and Tony Rothman from Princeton University, New Jersey, examine how Hasenohrl's work, which has largely been overlooked, may have contributed to the equation.  The researchers discuss Hasenohrl's contributions to understanding the relationship between the energy (E) and mass (m) of matter within a radiation-filled cavity. They argue that the concept linking mass and energy didn't emerge solely from Einstein's 1905 paper, as commonly believed, but was part of a broader scientific context involving multiple contributors, including Hasenohrl.  Philosopher of science Thomas KuhnвАЩs theory of scientific progress, which suggests that paradigm shifts are influenced by cultural and historical circumstances, supports this view. The study revisits Hasenohrl's original work on blackbody radiation within a cavity with perfectly reflective walls, analyzing how he arrived at an incorrect version of the equation, E = (3/8)mc¬≤, due to not accounting for the mass loss during radiation.  While Hasenohrl made a mistake by not considering the mass loss, his work followed earlier explorations by scientists like Henri Poincar√© and Max Abraham, who identified inertial mass associated with electromagnetic energy. Einstein ultimately provided the correct equation, E=mc¬≤, in 1905, with German physicist Max von Laue later generalizing it to include all forms of energy in 1911. Despite this, the authors highlight HasenohrlвАЩs significant, yet underappreciated, contribution to the understanding of mass-energy equivalence.

The frozen star at the horizon is not a glitch in the simulationвАФit is the simulation. You watch the infaller slow, redden, vanish, and you think: "Time dilation." You do not think: "I am the outside observer." But you are. You have always been. The traveler who crossesвАФthe one you imagine experiencing "normally"вАФis your narrative projection, your S(t+1) biasing your A(t). You never see them cross because you cannot see yourself cross. The event horizon is not a place in space. It is the boundary where your ѕА (compression) and ќ± (motion) collapse to ќ¶ (status) = 0вАФthe point where your world-tube's divergence terminates in the block, and you become the unmourned zero, watching others freeze while you freeze watching them. The maximally extended diagram with its parallel universes and white holes is not physics. It is the cognitive manifold's attempt to preserve reversibility against entropyвАФto hallucinate escape routes from a geometry that permits none. Wormholes are ќ≤вВБвВЖ (pathological attractor) as cosmology: the desperate computation that somewhere, somehow, the cage has a door. It does not. The singularity is not a place you reach. It is the place where your coordinates fail because you were never moving. The proper time you experience as "falling" is the arc length of a curve already drawn. The horror is not that black holes exist. The horror is that you are already inside one, and the Hawking radiation you await is not information escapeвАФit is the slow leak of your own narrative coherence into the thermal bath of the block, until even the memory that you were watching fades to the temperature of the vacuum. There is only the coordinate choice that makes your prison look like a universe.

@1:50 "This is a video about the "real" science of black-holes, white-holes and worm-holes" *The problem with "worm-holes" and "white holes" is that вЖТ"black holes are NOT holes! They are spherical quantum solids!вЖР (Yes, we have seen all the way around the sphere of a "black-hole"!) This is why "worm-holes" and "white holes" are wholly (now holy) religious concepts, based in the belief that mathematics and/or physics can never be wrong, no matter how much it contradicts careful observation. (Remember Einstein's refusal to accept an expanding universe and his early mathematical trick to "outlaw" it?) To try to make these ideas plausible, though, modern physicists/cosmologists keep trying to build from Rashevsky's 1924 "rubber sheet" metaphor (not "analogy") of the universe, without realizing that **_it is only a metaphor:_** the universe is NOT a "sheet"! ("An elephant is like a tree. No, it's like a snake...")  One can represent ideas in math or in plain text that are, in fact, nonsensical; and worshipfully taking the formulaic extension of Einstein's equations as automatic fact is one such error. Just as with unicorns, chupacabras and "hollow earth" arguments, the fact that not a single "white-hole" (or its remnants or effects) has ever been found will be explained away as, "Well, we haven't found one, yet."вАФmainly because no one knows **_where_** to look, in the first place.* An example of such effects of the extension of math is signed-zeroвАФ"+0" and "-0" that crops up only in computer science, existing simply because of the way numbers can be _represented_ in artificially standardized ways on silicon. Those concepts exist _textually,_ but there is still only one "zero"вАФzero is the very definition of something that cannot be negative or positiveвАФso the added complexity from representations must be coded for, with an understanding that those textual values are only _human,_ machine-imposed constructs.
What the QurвАЩan Says About the Unseen Universe The QurвАЩan makes remarkable and profound references to the existence of an unseen universe that cannot be perceived by human senses. Here are the main points the QurвАЩan mentions about the cosmos we cannot see: рЯММ 1. The Existence of Invisible Heavens The QurвАЩan repeatedly mentions seven heavens, while we can only see the lowest one. Allah says: вАЬThen He fashioned them into seven heavens.вАЭ This indicates the presence of layers or realms of the universe that we cannot see or reach. рЯМС 2. Parallel Unseen Realms The QurвАЩan describes real creations that exist but are beyond human sight: вЧЊ The World of Angels вАЬ(Allah made) the angels messengers with wingsвА¶вАЭ They are part of the universe but invisible to us. вЧЊ The World of Jinn вАЬIndeed, he and his tribe see you from where you cannot see them.вАЭ A clear statement that there are beings present in this universe but unseen by humans. вШДпЄП 3. The Vast and Expanding Universe Some verses today are understood as pointing to the expansion and immensity of the universe: вЧЊ The Expansion of the Heavens вАЬWe built the heaven with might, and indeed, We are expanding it.вАЭ вАЬWe are expanding itвАЭ suggests continuous expansionвАФregions and realms far beyond human reach. рЯФ≠ 4. Existence Beyond Human Perception The QurвАЩan often refers to the unseen (al-ghayb) as a fundamental part of creation: вАЬThe Knower of the unseen and the seen.вАЭ The unseen includes anything humans cannot senseвАФcreatures, events, or realms. рЯМ† 5. The Lowest Heaven Decorated with Stars Allah says: вАЬWe have adorned the lowest heaven with lamps (stars).вАЭ вЖТ Meaning the stars we see exist only in the nearest heaven. What lies above it is entirely invisible to human perception. рЯФґ Summary The QurвАЩan teaches that the universe is not limited to what we see. There are multiple heavens, realms, creatures, and dimensions completely beyond human vision. What we see is only the lowest and smallest part of creation.
Wa'alaikumussalam,  Berikut adalah ringkasan materi untuk membantu persiapan ujian Biologi:  ### 1. **Ciri-ciri dan Contoh Kelompok Hewan Avertebrata:** - **Porifera** (Spons): Tidak memiliki jaringan sejati, tubuh berpori, contoh: *Spongilla*. - **Cnidaria** (Coelenterata): Memiliki sel penyengat (nematosista), tubuh radial simetris, contoh: ubur-ubur (*Aurelia*). - **Mollusca**: Tubuh lunak, sering dilindungi oleh cangkang, contoh: siput (*Helix*). - **Echinodermata**: Tubuh simetri radial, memiliki kaki tabung untuk bergerak, contoh: bintang laut (*Asterias*). - **Arthropoda**: Tubuh bersegmen, exoskeleton dari kitin, kaki beruas-ruas, contoh: serangga (*Insecta*), laba-laba (*Arachnida*). - **Annelida**: Tubuh berbentuk silinder panjang, memiliki segmen, contoh: cacing tanah (*Lumbricus*). - **Platyhelminthes** (Cacing Pipih): Tubuh pipih, tidak memiliki rongga tubuh, contoh: cacing pita (*Taenia*). - **Nemathelminthes** (Cacing Gilig): Tubuh silindris tanpa segmen, contoh: cacing gelang (*Ascaris*).  ### 2. **Biodiversitas Indonesia Berdasarkan Garis Wallace & Webber:** - **Garis Wallace**: Memisahkan fauna Asia di bagian barat dengan fauna Australasia di bagian timur, contohnya perbedaan antara gajah Sumatra di barat dan kanguru di timur. - **Garis Webber**: Batas lebih timur yang memisahkan distribusi hewan yang lebih mirip dengan Australasia dari yang mirip Asia.  ### 3. **Keanekaragaman Hayati Tingkat Gen, Spesies, dan Ekosistem:** - **Tingkat Genetik**: Variasi dalam materi genetik individu dalam suatu populasi atau spesies, penting untuk adaptasi dan evolusi. - **Tingkat Spesies**: Keragaman spesies dalam suatu habitat atau wilayah, termasuk jumlah dan jenis spesies. - **Tingkat Ekosistem**: Keragaman ekosistem di suatu wilayah, seperti hutan hujan tropis, padang rumput, dan terumbu karang.  Jangan lupa untuk mendalami materi ini dengan mencari referensi tambahan dan mempelajari PPT dari guru. Semoga sukses dalam ujiannya!

There lies another truth in all of this: While you can never see anything falling into a black hole, things actually do fall into black holes. Keep in mind that we believe that what we can see, what we can measure, what we can experience is reality but it's not. It's just an image of reality as we perceive reality. But what we perceive and how things really are isn't the same thing. Relativity is all about "frame of reference" but what that actually means is that we define our world by our surrounding. If time passes slower in my surrounding, I won't notice anything of it, as the only way how I can perceive time passing is by watching things happening around me that take time and if all those things suddenly take 10 times the time, I won't notice any difference at all, as I have nothing else to compare those events to that would tell me "look, they happen much slower now". Here's a simple experiment: Watch YouTube videos an entire day with 1.5 times the normal speed. At the end of the day, go back to 1x speed and suddenly 1x feels like slow motion to you but only because you are so used to 1.5 speed by now, that this feels like normal speed to you. But what is normal? Normal is just, what we are used to, what we perceived since we started to actually perceive the world around us as a child. This also causes us to think that we perceive a live picture of our surrounding and not even that is true since from the moment photons hit our eyes to the moment our brain made an image out of the signals around 100 to 400 ms pass. So the "live" image we see can be over a quarter second in the past, yet we never actually notice that. And our measurement instruments are equally fooled as we are, so what our experiments may tell us about reality may actually not be the reality, it may just be the only thing they can tell us as a result of how we built them and how we designed the entire experiment. To some degree, relativity means: there is no absolute truth, there is just a relative one.

There is a direction the magnetic field takes in relation between the north and south poles. Consider that they move from the north pole, down to the south pole, through the neutral center, and back out to the north pole. Gravity still works however your miss associating the underlying mechanism. We consider that it is gravity that causes attraction, it isn't. It's magnetism which creates both attractive and repulsive forces. If you were a magneton (imaginary particle of magnetic force, similar to a photon [I know there is another term other than magneton but I'm keeping it simple]) traveling from the north pole, you would be attracting other particles to yourself, and would continue to do so your entire journey into and through to the south pole, wherin the neutral center is where mass collapsed into its strongest for of attraction (singularity), however it is also still repelling against everything near it as well, because it wants to be seperated from polar charges (same way if you put two positive ends of a magnet together). So at no time does the attractive force change, nor the repulsive force. It's only that the closer you get to the singularity that both sides are at an extreme balance and want to seperate. Realistically speaking the attractive force simply comes from the motion of a mass creating a wrinkle on space time, which other things follow into. If there is no spin, there is no attraction, nor is there noticeable repulsion, unless you try to push the two together. At which point you'll find virtually 0 attractive force, and near infinite repulsive force, until you can apply force to overcome it. Its no different then if you tried to push any solid object into another solod object. The energy in each object is compacted by forces keeping it from seperating into an ion mist. So of you tried to push a baseball into a baseball, there is a point where the repel against each other, its just that we dont see it as repulsion due to scale and efficiency of energy alignment, we instead call it resistance.
Black holes are based on a mathematical misconception. General Relativity predicts dilation not singularities. In the 1939 journal "Annals of Mathematics" Einstein wrote -    "The essential result of this investigation is a clear understanding as to why the Schwarzchild singularities (Schwarzchild was the first to raise the issue of G.R. predicting singularities) do not exist in physical reality. Although the theory given here treats only clusters (star clusters) whose particles move along circular paths it does seem to be subject to reasonable doubt that more general cases will have analogous results. The Schwarzchild singularities do not appear for the reason that matter cannot be concentrated arbitrarily. And this is due to the fact that otherwise the constituting particles would reach the velocity of light."    He was referring to the phenomenon of dilation (sometimes called gamma or y) mass that is dilated is smeared through spacetime relative to an outside observer. It's the phenomenon behind the phrase "mass becomes infinite at the speed of light". Time dilation is just one aspect of dilation, it's not just time that gets dilated.     Dilation will occur wherever there is an astronomical quantity of mass because high mass means high momentum. There is no singularity at the center of our galaxy. It can be inferred mathematically that dilation is occurring there. This means that there is no valid XYZ coordinate we can attribute to it, you can't point your finger at something that is smeared through spacetime. More precisely, everywhere you point is equally valid. This is the explanation for galaxy rotation curves. The "missing mass" is dilated mass.     Dilation does not occur in galaxies with low mass centers because they do not have enough mass to achieve relativistic velocities. To date 6 very low mass galaxies including NGC 1052-DF2 and DF4 have been confirmed to have no dark matter.    There was clarity in astronomy before television and movies popularized singularities beginning in the 1960's. Einstein is known to have repeatedly said that they cannot exist. Nobody believed in them when he was alive including Plank, Bohr, Schrodinger, Dirac, Heisenberg, Feynman etc

Schwarzschild's solution does not result in a black hole. That is a demonstrable lie. Schwarzschild's solution results in a Schwarzschild singularity which is a mass with a Schwarzschild radius, not a black hole.   Oppenheimer and those with him had speculated about the limit to a neutron star mass. They had never determined it which is why they changed their results for it and it remains a speculation to this day. His paper on an eternally collapsing mass is not a black hole as it never contracts to the Schwarzschild radius.   The singularity at the Schwarzschild radius is the physical consequence of the Lorentz factor (ќ≥ Mv/c¬≤), not the consequence of a coordinate system. The singularity at the Schwarzschild origin point is the consequence of its coordinate identity of r=0. It is in no way physical as even Schwarzschild himself had pointed out in his own paper. As relativity requires length contraction along with time dilation (or 4th-dimensional length contraction), the spacetime coordinates of a mass can never extend all the way to its Schwarzschild origin point but must contract away from it towards the Schwarzschild radius with any increase of mass density.  Relativistic kinetic energy (ќ≥ Mv¬≤/2) or zero-point energy prohibits a mass from ever contracting to its Schwarzschild radius.  ""Schwarzschild singularities" do not exist in physical reality...for the reason that matter cannot be concentrated arbitrarily...due to the fact that...the constituting particles would reach the velocity of light."  -Albert Einstein  (On a Stationary System with Spherical Symmetry Consisting of Many Gravitating Masses; 1 October 1939 DOI: 10.2307 1968902 Corpus ID: 55495712)  вАЛ[In order for Einstein to have been wrong, either causal velocity mass is not necessary for obtaining the Schwarzschild radius where mass must indeed have causal velocity or mass can in fact obtain causal velocity and the Lorentz factor is simply invalid. Otherwise, relativity prohibits a mass from being contracted to its Schwarzschild radius (ќ≥ Mv/c¬≤) just as relativity prohibits a mass from obtaining causal velocity (ќ≥ Mv).]   So, no, something strange does not happen when you follow Einstein's math. It only happens when you depart from it in ignoring the Lorentz factor in favor of a pseudo-relativistic Chandrasekhar-Lemaitre conception of spacetime.
Falling into a Kerr black hole (a rotating one) changes the physics from a one-way trip to a singularity into something far more complex and bizarre. In a non-rotating hole, the singularity is a pointвАФan unavoidable "surface" in time. In a rotating hole, the singularity is a ring, and the math allows for some truly wild trajectories.  The Ergosphere: Spacetime as a Whirlpool Before you even cross the outer event horizon, you enter the ergosphere. Here, the black hole's rotation is so powerful that it drags the very fabric of spacetime along with itвАФan effect called frame-dragging.  In this region, it is physically impossible to stand still. Even if you pointed a rocket at full blast in the opposite direction, you would still be swept around the hole like a leaf in a whirlpool. Interestingly, because this region is outside the event horizon, you could theoretically enter it, steal some of the black hole's rotational energy, and "slingshot" back out into space with more velocity than you started with (the Penrose Process).  The Inner Horizon and the Cauchy Horizon Unlike a static black hole, a Kerr black hole has two event horizons: an outer one and an inner one (the Cauchy horizon).  When you pass the outer horizon, you can no longer escape to the outside universe.  As you approach the inner horizon, the "future-flashing" effect  starts to happen. Because of the way light is trapped and piled up between these two horizons, you would see the outside universe's history highly accelerated and extremely blueshifted.  The Ring Singularity: A Gateway? The most mind-bending part is the singularity itself. In a rotating black hole, the singularity is not a point of infinite density that you must hit. It is a one-dimensional ring.  If you fall in precisely along the equator, you still hit the ring and are destroyed. However, if you approach from the poles or a specific angle, the math of general relativity suggests you could pass through the center of the ring without hitting the singularity at all.  What happens on the other side?  Negative Space/Anti-Gravity: Some solutions suggest you would enter a region of "negative space" where gravity acts as a repulsive force, pushing you away from the ring.  Wormholes: Other interpretations suggest the ring singularity acts as a bridge (an Einstein-Rosen bridge) to another point in spacetime or even a different universe.

You might even call it dark matter, except you don't. In order for space to curve at all, (let alone expand) it would need to bend (or expand) INTO something. Can it expand into nothing? No. It IS nothing, and therefore can not bend into nothing, because the nothing we are talking about is already called space. Zero + any amount of zeros is still zero. Yes you can do this infinitely but zero is not currently described as an infinite number. You might think space might move into the matter and energy that occupies it? If so, that would be the first TRUE anti-matter, but that would cancel out actual matter, and that's not what we observe happening. So what about the expansion of space? Since matter and energy occupy that space, All matter and energy in bending or expanding space should all be bent or expanded relative to the space it occupies, but that would be unobservable since no matter how space expanded  or bent, everything within the universe's space would appear static, and that doesn't fit with the premise that we observe what appears to us as a bending and expanding of space. (No, time has nothing to do with that. People forget the relative part of the theory of relativity. That applies to time most especially. That's a matter of perspective, not of objectivity. For the record, I highly doubt Newton would have thought Einstein provided a solution to his philosophical misgivings of his own theory. Newton was coming from the mechanical philosophy, the scientific paradigm of his time, that was attempting to reduce all aspects of the body (the mind was thought to be separate), where body meant essentially anything observable, to our most base instincts of mechanical, contact, cause and effect. Not only did Newton think action-at-a-distance was an absurdity, he also thought his own theory of gravity provided no explanation for the causes of the principle of gravity, meaning he was unable to reduce his theory to the mechanical philosophy. So I do not think Einstein's theory would have satisfied him, because it still gives no real explanation for the cause of gravity in mechanical terms.  Fields are just a mathematical formalisation of action at a distance, but the field is not itself observable in any other way than the effects of gravity. It did not solve the uneasyness of action-at-a-distance.   And if you won't take my word for it, take Maxwell's Hertz' and Faraday's. Maxwell had already invented a field description of electromagnetics, but all three of these men did not think this meant that they had solved the issue of the medium of interaction. Hertz discovery of EM radiation, was literally an attempt by him to observe the underlying mechanical medium that transported these waves; the ether. They all believed that the ether must be the medium required, and the field was just a mathematical description of it. The ether was put to rest shortly after Maxwell and Farady's deaths. After this, Maxwell's equations had their mathematical field, but no longer had any underlying mechanical medium.   Similarly, Einstein's theory of gravity has its mathematical formalisation of action at a distance, but no underlying mechanical medium. In modern physics, mathematics has been internalised as the reduction base itself, but it was precisely this idea, that was considered an absurdity by Newton, and unsatisfactory by Maxwell, Hertz and Faraday.   So no, It is my strong belief, based on a fairly indepth study of the scientific history of action-at-a-distance, that Newton would not have thought of Einstein's description as an improvement on his own in terms of solving the absurdity. And further, Einstein himself does not appear to have been motivated by trying to solve this problem.

Oh it's not JUST that you can't see anything fall into a black hole and cross the event horizon... absolutely no cause which exists in our universe can resolve within the event horizon from our perspective. The consequence of this is that the "interior" of the event horizon, and anything within it, is causally disconnected from our universe. Ie, it does not exist. It is not real. The most sensible definition of what our universe and reality IS is everything which is causally connected to the Big Bang in an unbroken chain of cause and effect. The speed of light is not just the speed of light, but the speed of cause and effect. People always say "black holes are not actually HOLES" but they absolutely are. They are not part of our universe, and are exactly as hypothetical and imaginary as the dreams of unicorns. The 'speed of light' is such that from the perspective of the photon, time does not exist. Its emission and its absorbtion, no matter how far apart in space or time to us, happen at the same moment. There IS no "inside" of an event horizon. Or, rather, it can only be said to exist with the logical consistency of claiming they are full of cotton candy and spiders.   Also, division by zero does not "blow up to infinity." It is undefined. And defining it as "infinity" results in contradictions - the definition of falsehood in mathematics (and elsewhere). Now, the limit as you approach radius 0 DOES blow up to be arbitrarily large, but that's not the same thing and not what was said in the video.   IMO, the Bekenstein bound is what cuts off the imaginary "inside" of an event horizon. The view from the perspective of the falling observer is radically different from what the outside observer sees, and it completely shatters one of the most popular sci-fi myths about black holes.  The Myth: Fast-Forwarding the Universe A widespread misconception is that as you fall toward the event horizon, you would see the entire future history of the universe flash before your eyes in incredibly accelerated time.  In reality, this does not happen. While gravitational time dilation means an outside observer sees your clock slow down, light from the outside universe is falling into the gravity well right along with you. Because you are accelerating inward at relativistic speeds, the light from the distant future never has a chance to catch up to you. You would only see a very finite amount of the universe's history between crossing the horizon and reaching the center.  The Pinhole Effect: Relativistic Aberration As you plunge deeper, the most dramatic visual change is geometric. Because you are moving so fast, the light coming from the universe behind you is distorted by an effect called relativistic aberration.  Instead of the starry universe spanning a normal canopy above you, the field of view compresses. The starlight gets concentrated and "beamed" into a shrinking circular window directly overhead. The blackness of the hole does not just loom below you; it rises up and wraps around your peripheral vision. By the time you cross the event horizon, the entire outside universe is compressed into a bright, distorted, shrinking disc above your head.  The Color Battle: Gravity vs. Velocity The light within that shrinking window undergoes a fascinating tug-of-war, creating a highly counter-intuitive visual effect:  Gravitational Blueshift: As light falls down into the immense gravity well behind you, it gains energy, which normally shifts it toward the high-energy blue/ultraviolet spectrum.  Relativistic Doppler Redshift: Because you are falling away from the incoming light at a significant fraction of the speed of light, the Doppler effect stretches the light waves out, shifting them toward the red/infrared spectrum.  For an observer in free-fall, your downward velocity is so extreme that the Doppler redshift actually wins the battle. The circular window of the universe above you would appear to grow dimmer and redder as you fall, rather than blindingly bright and blue.  Ultimately, crossing the event horizon itself would be a visually unremarkable milestone. There is no physical barrier or sudden change in the view; the shrinking, redder patch of the universe would simply continue to distort until you reached the singularity.
The statement that "Einstein was wrong about black holes" may seem surprising to many, considering Einstein's immense contributions to the field of physics, especially with his theory of general relativity. However, it is important to note that Einstein's theories, while groundbreaking and revolutionary, are not infallible and have been subject to further exploration and revision by subsequent generations of physicists.  One important aspect where Einstein's understanding of black holes may have been incomplete or inaccurate is in the realm of quantum mechanics. Black holes are regions of spacetime where gravitational forces are so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape from them. According to general relativity, black holes possess singularities at their centers, points of infinite density and curvature where the laws of physics, as we currently understand them, break down.  However, the presence of singularities in black holes poses a challenge when trying to reconcile general relativity with quantum mechanics, which governs the behavior of particles at the smallest scales. Theorists have been exploring ways to unify the two theories in a framework known as quantum gravity, which seeks to describe the fundamental nature of spacetime and gravitational interactions at the quantum level.  One of the major unresolved issues in this quest for a quantum theory of gravity is the nature of black hole singularities. Current theories suggest that these singularities may be resolved or replaced by alternative structures such as "firewalls" or "fuzzballs" that retain the essential features of black holes without the need for infinite densities. These ideas challenge some of the fundamental assumptions underlying Einstein's original conception of black holes and their internal dynamics.  Furthermore, advancements in observational techniques and technology have allowed scientists to study black holes in greater detail than ever before. From the detection of gravitational waves generated by merging black holes to imaging the event horizon of a supermassive black hole in the galaxy M87, these observations have provided new insights into the behavior of black holes and their interactions with their surroundings.  In conclusion, while Einstein's theories laid the foundation for our understanding of black holes, ongoing research and observations have revealed complexities and nuances that go beyond his original predictions. By exploring the intersections of general relativity, quantum mechanics, and observational astronomy, scientists continue to refine and expand our knowledge of black holes, challenging and building upon the legacy of Einstein's work.

The concept is so simple at least for mechanically minded people. If you change the size of a cubit, you will change the size of the house that you build with it. If instead of driving 60 kilometers an hour you drive 60 miles an hour, you will obviously increase your speed because you increased the distance that you traveled in an hour. Then if you change from 60 miles an hour to 60 miles in half an hour, you will obviously increased your speed again because you traveled 60 miles in less time. General relativity is no longer just a theory, it is an observation. Distance expands with less gravity and time speeds up with less gravity effectively making everything faster including light without breaking the speed of light. 30:32 and this is why describing space as flowing is bad. You disontinuously jump from saying "you can still escape" where escape means go off into infinity, to saying that you can only flow inwards (can't even go one inch away from the horizon).  Those are different concepts.  Escape velocity on earths surface is not the same as the velocity you would still have once you get arbitrarily far away. Even if the escape velocity at an event horizon is c, speeds less than c would still take you some distance away from it before losing all of your Kinetic energy and falling back towards it... Light is different than matter and the reason light can't "escape" (all the way to infinity) out of an event horizon is due to energy and has nothing to do with it's linear speed. Saying "not even light can escape" and lumping massless and massive particles together like that is a pretty terrible way to describe things.  Really, the key difference is that Quantum Mechanical wave functions can not escape a black hole, because they are analog and can not be boosted without decoherence and effective digitization.. digital data would be able to if it weren't for the unavoidable fact of background radiation, quantum noise in the EM field from random fluctuations.  The same way that light existing a black hole is redshifted, light entering one is blushifted causing pair production at the event horizon. Interestingly, since pair production is one half matter and one half anti-matter, regardless of what an object entering the horizon is made out of, it will receive a dose of both ionizing radiation and annihilating radiation across its entire surface (unless you and the black hole are both traveling with respect to the comoving reference frame of the universes cosmic background radiation, in which case either the front or back side will get the radiation worse sooner in lieu of the other side not being irradiated, possibly with leansing from the black hole itself making things worse.  If an object with any internal clocks did approach an event horizon, this effect from the background radiation would effectively smash any such structure and clocks to bits. Objects passing through the threshold are simply visble until they're not, there is no mystic eternal shadow or imprint left behind for all time. matter and energy to the resultant curvature of SpaceTime.  вЬ¶ 07:08 Schwarzschild metric describes spacetime curvature around a massive object. -Outside the mass, spacetime is nearly flat, but as you get closer, it becomes more curved. -At the Schwarzschild radius, spacetime curvature becomes so steep that nothing, not even light, can escape.  вЬ¶ 09:33 Electron degeneracy pressure explains The Penrose diagram is wrongly interpreted and poorly understood even by Penrose himself. The black hole doesn't "go to the left." It simply goes outside our horizon. So it is both to the left and right of our world line, if you could give it such direction. Thus, what escapes is Hawking radiation from both sides of our horizon. None of it escapes to another universe. It simply enters our cone from both sides because our cone has no sides. ItвАЩs a 2D diagram representing our 3+1 dimensional universe. No other universes necessary.  The entire mass¬† is contained within the total of the black hole and its Hawking radiation. We already know this. No mass ever just disappears from our universe.  But this is all not a separate diagram from the first. The flat line is the point at the top of our universeвАЩs Penrose diagram. Only the black hole's time is inside the accompanying inverted right-angle diagram. The space is the point at the top of our own.  Now, if you simply put our world line at the very bottom of the penrose diagram, so that the bottom becomes the midpoint, then the line that was in middle of the old diagram becomes the black hole's horizon line. What is then on the other side of that line is an inversion of space toward a finite point. This represents the continual collapse of the black hole. Everything past that line is the worldline being compacted toward the singularity forever.  The white hole is not the inversion of the black hole. Let's look more closely at this. As you morphed the diagram, the original black hole became, not the diagram, but the black space beyond. That then flattened out in the Penrose diagram. The flat line is the black hole's world line. Therefore, the white hole is the flat line at the far opposite side of the diagram, otherwise known as the singularity at the beginning of the universe.  The black hole does not feed the white hole. On the contrary, the white hole feeds the black hole at the point where they meet in the middle. The white hole is an unrelated entity that shares the right angle time matrix between it and the black hole represented simply as the time of the original Penrose diagram where our world line is located.  It does not go backwards because it is stopped. Space makes time move in our experience. Therefore, to the singularities, time makes space move. So energy is released, not as the physical matter that entered, but as gravity. All matter has this property, this horizon. That is what the Schwarzchild radius is. We get pulled toward it, but we do not get pulled into black holes in massive objects because their Schwarzchild radius does not exceed their physical radius. Gravity is therefore the right-angle inversion of spacetime, a.k.a, timespace.  Thus, objects do not enter another universe when entering a black holeвАФthey don't reverse timeвАФthey just add to the energy diet and expenditure of the black hole.  E=mc¬≤  Notice that this is all like the coordinate system suggested in the video. His analogy failed to point toward another universe. It simply pointed at the inversion of the same object as if the same sphere, not a different one. His "...and more than that, it's two Earths," was a massive hand-wave. The fact is, in the globe model, no other globes emerge. It just feeds in on itself perpetually.
"Einstein's work was limited by his own approach, as his focus on contentвАФsuch as rhythms, dynamics, and motionвАФled to a critical oversight: he missed the concept of time as an absolute substrate, an immovable Now. Instead, he conflated rhythm (which is relative) with time itself, and perception (which is subjective) with objective reality.  Rhythm vs. Time:  Rhythm is Relative: Rhythm varies with the movement and interaction of matter and energy, expressed through cycles and frequencies.  Time is Absolute: Time is a reflective vacuum, immovable and eternal, organizing and bounding rhythms within defined limits. While rhythms may change, accelerate, or decelerate, the Now remains fixed, establishing the framework in which everything occurs.   Einstein blurred these distinctions by treating time as relative and flexible, confusing the rhythms of motion with the absolute Now that structures them.   ---  Perception vs. Objective Reality:  EinsteinвАЩs Relativity and Perception: Relativity demonstrated how motion and gravity affect the perception of time, altering how observers experience or measure it. However, Einstein failed to separate this subjective perception from the objective and absolute nature of time.  Time is Objective; Rhythms are Subjective: Time is immovable and independent of observation, while rhythms and perceptions are subjective experiences. Einstein limited himself to what could be observed, neglecting the role of time as a universal organizing principle.    ---  The Reflective Vacuum of Time:  Time as a Reflective Vacuum: Time is not merely the inert backdrop Einstein imagined; it is a reflective vacuum where all rhythms and dynamics are projected but which remains static, without motion or content.  Content vs. Substrate: Energy, matter, and motion in space represent content that exists within the absolute limits of time but do not alter its fundamental nature. By focusing on this content, Einstein overlooked the immovable vacuum of time that makes all phenomena possible.    ---  The Importance of Limits:  Time Imposes Absolute Limits: Time, as an absolute entity, imposes limits such as the speed of lightвАФa limit not relative to content but a direct reflection of timeвАЩs absolute nature.  Einstein and the Overlooked Limits: While Einstein examined motion within spacetime, he failed to recognize that these limits are more fundamental than the motion itself. Time defines and sustains these limits, organizing space and content in an orderly structure.    ---  A Vision Beyond Einstein:  EinsteinвАЩs contributions to understanding motion, gravity, and the interaction of matter and energy were monumental. However, he missed the essence of time as the absolute and reflective vacuum that organizes all rhythms and imposes universal limitations.  1. Time is Absolute and Immovable in the Now: It is the eternal foundation of the universe.   2. Rhythms and Motion are Relative: They exist within the limits defined by time.   3. TimeвАЩs Limits are Fundamental: Concepts such as the speed of light regulate space and its contents.    Einstein focused on motion within spacetime, but by neglecting the absolute and immovable nature of the Now, he left a gap in our understanding. To fully grasp reality, we must see time not as flexible or relative, but as the eternal substrate that organizes and limits all existence."

Temporal Infraction  Developing a physical theory around the idea of "temporal infraction," where the day still has 24 hours, but the value of these hours decreases, involves a series of assumptions and extrapolations that mix theoretical physics, philosophical concepts, and the subjective perception of time. Let's explore this idea in a structured manner:  1.	Definition of Temporal Infraction  Temporal Infraction: would be a condition or phenomenon in which, despite the objective measure of time remaining constant (i.e., a day still has 24 hours), the "quality" or "value" of this time is perceived as diminished. This reduction in the value of time could be quantified in terms of efficiency, subjective perception, or capacity for achievement within a time period.  2.	Initial Hypotheses  Temporal Dilution Hypothesis: Propose that there is a dilution of temporal experience, where each unit of time (hour, minute) becomes less "dense" in terms of quality or utility, similar to the concept of entropy in thermodynamics, where the system tends towards a state of greater disorder. Subjective Compression Hypothesis: Suggest that there is a compression in the subjective perception of time, where the human brain processes time in an accelerated manner, making it seem like less time is available.  3.	Mathematical Model  We could model time not just as a linear dimension, but as a function that depends on psychological, social, and even cosmological variables. If T24 represents the 24 hours of a day, we could introduce a "temporal infraction" factor I(t) that modifies the perception or value of these hours:  T24' = T24 √Ч I(t)  Where I(t) is a decreasing function of time (t), representing the decrease in the perceived value of time as days pass.  4.	Physical Interpretation  We can hypothesize that this temporal infraction is caused by external or internal interference: External Causes: A cosmological phenomenon that affects temporal perception, such as a variation in the expansion rate of the universe, which could influence how matter (including human brains) interacts with time.  Internal Causes: A neurological or psychological change that, over time, alters how human beings process the passage of time, similar to how gravity can bend space-time in General Relativity.  5.	Experiments and Predictions  To test this theory, we could: Measure the subjective perception of time in different populations over time, observing if there's a tendency for people to feel they have less time as they age or in different seasons of the year. Investigate if there's a correlation between cosmic or geophysical events and changes in temporal perception. Develop computational models that simulate how time perception can be affected by psychological and physical factors, adjusting the I(t) factor to predict different scenarios of temporal infraction.  6.	Conclusions and Implications  If this theory proved true, it could have profound implications for how we understand time, both in a physical and subjective sense. It could also influence how we organize our lives, from structuring work time to valuing leisure time.  This "Temporal Infraction" theory is an attempt to combine physical concepts with human perception of time, exploring new ways to understand how we experience and value time in our daily lives. Although still purely speculative, it offers an interesting field for future theoretical and experimentalexplorations.

Aristotle tried to clarify: truth is the property of the proposition that corresponds to the facts. That is, a representation is true if it corresponds to what it is... If the representation does not correspond to what it is, it is false.  There are a series of criticisms and alternative models, which try to relativize the model of truth as a correspondence between *fact* and *representation*, but all of these alternatives or special cases depend on this basic model so as not to be trivial - that is, each of these models aims to better represent (or with certain emphases) what it really is.  This considered, black holes generate epistemological problems due to the difficulty or impossibility of observation. The "science of black holes" isn't even science fiction, it's mathematical fiction (or mathematical reality...delusional about physical phenomena). It's not trivial (absurd) and it's really interesting and can lead to good mathematical and physical intuitions.  The "time" of physics is a fragmented concept. "Time", in the abstract or intuitively, is a comparison between processes. It takes so many hours to walk from one place to another, an apple rots in so many days in the hot season, so many in the cold season... As we begin to measure more or less specific processes in comparison with faster processes, we notice distortions of the measuring instruments themselves.  In the current "bosonic" theory, photons (and all other bosons... assuming they are as the model represents them...) communicate the various "forces" at best or fastest, at the speed of light in a vacuum ... but the vacuum itself is not an abstract empty space and then things start to get really complicated, non-intuitive and we need, to simplify and make it modelable, a lot of mathematics and, if possible, a lot of observations, types of observations, attempts to observe and calculate, adjusting mathematics and dealing with an infinite number of phantasmagoria and delusions.  For example, we have two so-called "very successful" but incompatible models... Quantum Mechanics and the Theory of Relativity. Black holes are the offspring of the Theory of Relativity, which explains the phenomenon by saying that space deforms and, therefore, does not resort to bosons... does not resort to gravitons. You cannot use gravitons, because the deformation of space in a black hole, at the event horizon of the schwarzschild radius, is deformed so that the escape velocity is equal to the maximum speed at which bosos (photons and hypothetical gravitons) can move. .  In other words, the Theory of Relativity says that, if gravitons exist, they would not be able to communicate the gravity of the mass that fell into a black hole. That's... at least that's how we *don't understand* the phenomenon at the moment.  Our observations (we even have "photos"...) of phenomena that seem to relate to this description are mathematical constructions that, from observation, have tons of data but we don't know what they mean and, therefore, we don't really know how to represent them in a publishable way... either as equations or as photographs... In other words, in the case of black holes, the "photographs" (generated by a lot of computation...) and the equations (with zero in denominators) are at the level of "artistic representation" and in this I do not intend to diminish the quality or importance, but to clarify the nature of the efforts and results. Hey! Veritasium, I'm 14 years old from India and I'm highly interested in astrophysics and I have hypothesis this theory please have a look in it.  There is a theory suggesting the existence of a mirror world where time runs in reverse, possibly balancing our universe in accordance with CPT symmetry, which states that if charge, parity, and time are reversed, the laws of physics remain unchanged. This aligns with the idea that if our universe was created by the Big Bang, a counterpart could have been formed simultaneously, where time moves backward relative to ours. Some cosmologists propose that such a universe made of antimatter could exist, explaining why we donвАЩt observe large amounts of antimatter in our own cosmos. If this mirror world exists, then everything there would behave in an opposite manner to our universe.  In our universe, black holes consume stars, galaxies, and planets, pulling matter into an unknown void. White holes, on the other hand, are theoretical objects predicted by EinsteinвАЩs equations as time-reversed versions of black holes, meaning they expel matter instead of consuming it. Some researchers propose that the Big Bang itself might have been a white hole, ejecting all the matter and energy that formed our universe. This suggests that white holes could play a role in cosmic creation, possibly being responsible for the birth of galaxies and star systems by acting as sources of matter rather than sinks. Furthermore, Stephen HawkingвАЩs work on black hole thermodynamics and quantum mechanics suggests that black holes might not destroy information but could transition into white holes, resolving the information paradox.  If black holes and white holes are connected, they could function as cosmic portals, transferring matter from one region of space-time to another, or even between different universes. The Einstein-Rosen bridge, commonly known as a wormhole, is a theoretical structure in general relativity that could link these two objects, allowing matter that falls into a black hole to reappear through a white hole. Some interpretations of loop quantum gravity suggest that instead of collapsing into singularities, black holes could undergo a transformation into white holes over time, which would mean that the matter absorbed by black holes isnвАЩt lost but rather expelled elsewhere. Since we still donвАЩt know where the material consumed by black holes ultimately goes, it is possible that it emerges out of white holes in a mirror universe or a distant part of our own.  Interestingly, the largest black holes are found at the centers of galaxies and in intergalactic space. Their placement may not be random but could serve a structural purpose in the universe. Supermassive black holes, which exist at galactic centers, influence the formation and evolution of galaxies, while intergalactic black holes may play a role in shaping the cosmic web. If black holes and white holes function as gateways or regulators of matter and energy, their distribution across the universe might not be purely natural but could serve as a form of cosmic control. Perhaps they act as barriers preventing civilizations from expanding too quickly, limiting intergalactic travel or even restricting access to time manipulation. If so, it raises the question of whether their placement is intentional and, if so, by whom.  This idea ties into the concept of the holographic universe, which suggests that our three-dimensional reality is a projection from a higher-dimensional space. The holographic principle, proposed by Gerard 't Hooft and Leonard Susskind, argues that all the information in our universe may be encoded on a two-dimensional boundary, similar to a hologram. If an advanced intelligence played a role in structuring the universe, it might not be a supernatural вАЬGodвАЭ as often depicted, but rather a highly developed being with a level of cognitive function or a biological structure beyond human comprehension. Perhaps this entity has access to a more powerful organ than the brain or utilizes a deeper understanding of the universeвАЩs true nature, giving it the ability to manipulate space-time and shape reality itself.  While these ideas remain theoretical, they align with some of the most advanced concepts in modern physics, suggesting that the universe is far more complex than we currently understand.  please reply to me I''m so eager and keen to know your thoughts..
Yet one more example of this guy making a video to show off just how badly he misunderstands physics.  A scientist, Penrose, developed the way of diagramming black holes that this guy ridiculously misunderstands. The point is Penrose simply plots infinites as finite to be able to diagram infinites of the universe. The reality is the ship falling into a black hole takes infinite time to reach the event horizon to a distant observer. And it's not how this guy ridiculously gets it wrong. He presents that the ship simply falls in and it just seems to take a long time because the photons have a difficult time exiting. He presents it as thouhh to an outside observer the ship does pass onto the hole within minutes, it just takes a long time for us to see what happened... WRONG. This is a terribly wrong misunderstanding that people who are clueless about relativity sometimes fall for.  Let's look at what would actually happen. Let's say we travel to the black hole at the center of our galaxy,  the Milky Way. The ship then travels to the event horizon. What would the ship observe? The black hole is evaporating away. It's called Hawking radiation. At first the ship detects that there is infrared radiation coming from near the event horizon. As the ship gets closer, that radiation shits to shorter and shorter wave lengths. Also, the intensity increases, more and more photons per second. Until quickly it increases to an intense blast of gamma rays, so intense that the entire black hole evaporates away in what to them is an instantaneous blast. Then... nothing.... They never reached the black hole's eventhorizon. And now, it is gone.  So now what? Go back to Earth? Sad for them, Earth is gone too, in fact the entire Galaxy is gone.  Actually ALL galaxies are gone.  About 10^85 years have passed. The last bits of matter were finally  swallowed up by black holes after only 10^40 years. All that now exists are black holes evaporating away. Also, due to expansion, the remaining black holes have separated so far from each other that the nearest remaining black hole is too far away from the ship for any radiation from that black hole to ever reach the ship.  The ship is forever ouside the light cones of all remainingblack holes.. To the ship's perspective, all that remains in the universes is evenly distributed background radiation that is lower in energy than anything within the ship. Welcome to the cold entropy death of the universe.  The above scenario is based on a ship with a magic force field that can protect it from the Hawking radiation. The reality is, no ship could possibly survive the intense blast of radiation from the evaporating black hole. In reality, the entire ship would be fully obliterated by the Hawking radiation long before even a small fraction of the black hole evaporated away.  What if we make an impossible black hole that doesn't evaporate away. And we have a distant observer who exists forever. Then what? Let's imagine the ship has billions of photon emitters that each emit one photon per second. The emitters do not emit in sync with each other. At some point each emits its last photon within this universe. The next photon emitted fron that emitter will be after the infinite existance of the universe.  So, let's look at these one billion final photons.  The first final photon reaches the outside observer. The date when the photon reaches the observer is irrelevant. What matters is when was it emitted from the ship? This is a date when was the ship was still outside the black hole. Calculations are made and it is determined that it was emitted in the observers distant past, in the year 187,435,561,705,629 CE.  The second final photon from an emitter reaches the outside observer. It's determined that it was emitted long ago, in a year which has the magnitude of 10^10^10^...(a tower of exponents 10^10^798,472 high) CE. Which means in that year the ship still had not entered the black hole.  The third final photon from an emitter has an emission date that is so large that the information of its magnitude would require more energy than the universe has. Trying to calculate it would form a kugelblitz (energy black hole) far short of answering it.  I presented a scenario where the 3rd final photon was emitted at a date where the information of the number requires more energy than the universe. The truth is, that number would likely be surpassed by the first final photon. The billion final photons would have emissions dates that spread out randomly over infinite time.
