The Universe as a Dynamic Information System Imagine the universe as a vast, interconnected information system, where everythingвАФfrom particles to galaxiesвАФrepresents data, and the laws of physics are the algorithms governing how this data interacts and evolves. Here's how this analogy unfolds:  1. Cosmic Code: The Laws of Physics as Algorithms The universe operates according to a set of fundamental rules, like a program running on a cosmic computer. These "algorithms" include the laws of physics (like gravity, electromagnetism, quantum mechanics), which process and organize information at every scale. Example: Just as a computer processes input data to produce output, the gravitational algorithm "processes" the mass and energy of objects to determine how they move through spacetime. 2. Particles and Forces as Bits of Information Elementary particles, such as quarks, electrons, and photons, can be seen as the "bits" of the universeвАФdiscrete units of information. Forces like gravity and electromagnetism are the protocols that define how these bits interact, exchange, and transform. Example: A photon (light particle) traveling from a star to your eye is like a data packet moving through the cosmic network, carrying information about the starвАЩs position, temperature, and composition. 3. Space and Time as the Operating System Space and time provide the framework within which all cosmic processes occur, analogous to an operating system on which programs run. This "operating system" isn't static; it's dynamic, shaped by the presence of mass and energy (as described by general relativity). Example: When a massive object like a planet warps spacetime, itвАЩs akin to an operating system adjusting its parameters based on active processesвАФslowing down clocks or altering paths of nearby objects. 4. Entropy and Complexity: The UniverseвАЩs Data Processing As the universe evolves, it processes information, increasing in complexity. Entropy, a measure of disorder, reflects the way information is distributed and transformed, driving the universe from simple beginnings (like the homogeneous early universe) to the complex structures we see today (like galaxies and life forms). Example: The formation of stars and galaxies can be viewed as the universe organizing information into more complex structures, just as data can be organized into increasingly intricate patterns. 5. Quantum Mechanics: The Code's Uncertainty At the quantum level, the universeвАЩs "code" incorporates uncertainty and probability, leading to outcomes that are not deterministic but probabilistic. This is like a program that, instead of giving a single output, generates a range of possibilities depending on certain parameters. Example: The behavior of an electron in an atom, which can exist in multiple states simultaneously (superposition), resembles a system processing multiple lines of code simultaneously before collapsing into a final result. 6. The Universe as a Self-Learning System The universe could be seen as a self-learning system, constantly adjusting and fine-tuning its parameters. This could explain why the laws of physics seem so finely tuned for the existence of complex structures like galaxies, stars, and life. Example: Over time, the universe "learns" which configurations of matter and energy are stable and conducive to complexity, akin to how a neural network in AI adjusts its weights to improve performance. 7. Consciousness as Emergent Information Processing Conscious beings like us might represent the universeвАЩs most advanced form of information processing. Our brains are complex networks that interpret, process, and respond to vast amounts of data, mirroring the broader information-processing activities of the universe. Example: Human thought and consciousness could be seen as the universe reflecting on itself, a localized concentration of information processing that has become self-aware. Final Thought: The Universe as a Cosmic Web of Interconnected Data In this view, the universe is not a static collection of objects but a dynamic, evolving network of information. The structure of reality is woven from the interactions and exchanges of data, with the laws of physics acting as the protocols that guide this cosmic computation.  This perspective emphasizes the interconnectedness of all thingsвАФhow every particle, wave, and field is part of a vast, interwoven tapestry of information. The universe, in this sense, is a self-organizing, self-regulating system, constantly processing, evolving, and learning, with life and consciousness as its most sophisticated expressions.  This conceptual approach provides a holistic way of thinking about the universe, blending the rigor of scientific laws with the fluidity of information theory and the creativity of imagination.

At the centre of a singularity space and time can have a second event horizon where the mass of the singularity pulls out in equal opposite directions, this is where we sit, in a tiny area of spacetime and as the singularity evaporates it cools and expands, space and time dilates so it seems constant, but the rules of the board change as we expand into the 4th dimension at the speed of relativity.   If i had a magic remote, where i could manipulate spacetime, i could pause my dilation and rewind the universe, in 13.5 billion years it all comes back to look like a singularity right in front of me, but if i didnt pause my dilation i would have shrank with the wave.. from an observers perspective I grew in the 4th dimension as time was reversed until i was larger than the observable universe and my light was red shifted to oblivion... If I simply set off now in any direction at the speed of light it would take me 46 billion light years to reach the big bang, the shorter path is that journey in 4D space, i just need to expand to travel back, or shrink to travel forward in time, once I get there i should fall onto my right orbital path for my speed in the 4th dimension, just like changing orbits is a matter of adding more energy in space to change orbital lines. it should work the same in the dimension of time. its hard to explain....  Imagine if you shrank down tho so a hydrogen atom was the size of a star, the universe outside would red shift to oblivion, it would be like time travel forward in time..  the IR lightwaves of the atom blue shifted from your perspective, it might look like a plasma ball in space rather than a collection of buckey balls around a proton.. If you was larger than a galaxy then a star would feel as warm to you as an LED light bulb.. scale and perspective seem to be everything in the universe.. there doesnt seem to be an absolute limit to things.. how do you keep cutting an atom in half, when its just a rippling wave of energy in quantum dynamics..  its just field energy... Just running on different scales and perspectives, its the same math that predicts a puddle of water evaporating and uranium decay, if you tweak C.. we use water to model supersonic air because it acts the same way... tick rates change a solid into a liquid or gas, look at the earth sped up fast enough and its surface is moving like a semi molten pool of magma  The reason you feel gravity isnt quirks of curved spacetime, its 4th dimensional translation. Apply a 1% translation in both X Y and Z axes, and you wont see any difference out there as everything scales the same, but you, the planet, the stars and universe all expand at different rates, out here on the surface of the larger object you feel stuck to it as it races through the 4th dimension, at the core the translational effect is null, you expand equally with the planet and feel no gravity, this is why you feel like you fall at 9.8mtr/s you arent falling, it is expanding that much further than you.. in the 4th dimension. Being at the centre of any massive object, you are weightless to its dilation in spacetime, and the translational forces are equal.. the further you get from that central point, the more gravity you feel as spacetime tries to force you there..   makes more sense than curved space makes your legs tick faster in special relativity no?  What you see around you is all curved spherical by that 4th dimension, but a flat straight curved line in time is flat in spacetime.. the 3D earth is conical, a 4D earth is spherical, i can do to the earth what Minkowski and Einstein did to light, turn it into a light cone and you see curved orbits for the straight paths in curved space that they are.. take your radius as the cones height and circumference as the diameter on top and you mapped mass in the dimension of time, anything with mass will do orbits, light not so much.. its our yard stick but its not running on the same rules entirely.. if it did have mass and could do orbits you would be convinced the universe was way flatter than you do now.. of course it would be real dark..  Unlike Minkowskis or Einsteins universes mine doesnt flip when the cones get to infinity, the board just keeps scaling, an orbit of the observable universe would take just as long now, as it would 13 billion years ago, at relativistic speeds.. an orbit being the only true constant in the universe.. it can scale as small or as large as it wants, as long as information doesnt travel faster than the speed of light neither event horizon will ever be reached, we can expand or contract eternally in finite space.  The big bang needs dark energy to explain the accelerating universe. mine not so much, as the singularity evaporates the universe relaxes and everything expands to fill the space, its simple really..  A universe void of energy or mass is void of time..  so either its all flat or nothing is. its just weirdly spherical in nature, not cubic as we like to imagine.. straight lines arent really a thing out here, nothing is flat straight or parallel for long, we can see the same supernova happen multiple times a year as its light takes different routes from source to destination, even in the void of space.. we need to remember what you see is 4 dimensions, not 3..
Like Newton, Einstein didn't know what gravity or gyroscopic inertia was. Gyroscopic inertia is motion at a speed of 38 million times the speed of light. Einstein claimed it was the speed of light, while Newton claimed it was infinite. As we see, virtue lies in the middle.  Perfect universes rotate between 38 million and 20 billion times the speed of light, which will allow for the specific amount of atomic mass in the elements necessary for biological life to exist in perfect universes. Einstein's relativity doesn't know the difference between time (greatness), which is motion, and measured time, which is a measure of greatness, with clocks or any other device. For this reason, there is a difference between the two, and it is obvious; that's why errors are absurd.  Einstein's relativity doesn't know the difference between atomic mass at rest, if rest exists since everything in the universe is in motion, even when it appears to be at rest, and inertial atomic mass. For the same body in motion, both are considered equal by science, and this was a serious mistake when calculating black holes, since they do not actually have the gravity or gyroscopic inertia that grants them. I do not concur with your explanation involving the lightcones.. Even though it is beautifully animated and seems to explain it all.. It is compliant with the current theories, it follows from Einsteins physics.. yes, I do not contest that.. but... Einsteins theorem rests upon a never defined axiom that no information travels faster than light, which keeps cause and effect and the arrow of time, entropy and whatnot.. all the trappings that allow experience at all.. However this is just an axiom. So is there any other way to see it.. I propose the following:  see macrocosmic events and laws of physics as emerging from quantum scales and possibly beyond (and influenced possibly by macro-macroscales, but let¬іs not overcomplicate, everything emerges from everything else).. The point is: existence is essentially chaotic in this worldview. And we learn from studying chaotic (or non-linear dymanic) systems, that they often "flip" to a new attractor as a result of a relatively minor influence. So the laws that we currently enjoy, might very well ќЕflip" at any moment. Like the earth magnetic poles may flip at any moment. Imagine the arrow of time flipping.. This means cause and effect are by no means a guarantee.. It might be a completely local phenomenon. In fact it is more likely to be local than global... If the quantum-scale rests upon an even smaller scale, this becomes even more likely.. Problem is, is there is a non-causal event anywhere in history, the whole thing gets patchy and full of holes.. Now given what I told you do you think is the chance of no non-causal event whatsoever, anywhere in infinite history... It is a bit too much of a closed loop and I feel we miss out.. For instance, time/space is a continuum, but there would be no time without event, there would be no space without mass or energy.. So... we are missing part of our continuum, it is space/time/ density . else it wouldn¬іt be there at all.  Density of what?? Well information? Density of existence.. your guess is as good as mine.. But since information is a very likely candidate and it all rest on information not traveling faster than light, you must see my problem.. Also adding density adds pattern in space and time, making there not here and then not now.. So it is very strange to expect everything to just stay the same for ever and be as it is here everywhere.. This is not the generally accepted view, but it is a valid one. And I think it satisfies occam¬іs razor at least.. If there is only space/time, what is everything IN spacetime?? It must be there, why is it standard to leave it OUT of the continuum and suppose we have something new and undetermined? Makes no sense. String theory seemed a nice explanation for a while, but that didn¬іt pan out. There is very likely a major irregulariy in the standard model as well.. Isn¬іt it time to look at other paradigms, that can encompass our old ones?  Anyway, this changes how you see black holes, because they are infinitely dense or at least something approaching infinitely dense, this would result in infinite universes spawning here, if only there was time and space for them to spawn in.. But as density grows, the amount of available time and space goes down..  This space-time-density continuum may also explain dark matter.. If space-time-density is the actual continuum, is there a real reason for regions of space to not be denser AND ALSO contain more time and space (ok, awkward to talk about regions containing space, but I hope you get what I mean).. Dark matter would essentially be a misty black hole :).. It also means what you said: the inside of a black hole is the end of time for anything.. Or the beginning, anyway outside of time and space.. Since infitnite density can also be seen as infinite scarcity of space-time. There is just not enough space and time for anything to happen in. Given this continuum, we have one more obligation, which is to define what happening actually is.. I think we might define it as is a grouping of time (an event) given the same or relatively low contrast in density and space.. My high mind   "Can we model a singularity as an elastic sheet that constantly attempts to return to flat, but its mode of expressing that elasticity is by generating MORE of itself, instead of pulling on existing material? So that instead of a waterfall, where space is moving towards the black hole, instead space is expanding, under your feet AHEAD of you as you run away from the black hole?"      It struck me to be oddly congruent with the "expansion" of the universe, and a potentially unique way to look at the effect mass itself might have on propelling the expansion of the universe. It could be logically incongruent with observed effects, but I'm not familiar enough with the actual literature to have a good instinct there.     Anyone know if this is explored as a model? That mass essentially "eats" spacetime, which is what energy actually is, the curvature created by space continuously propagating itself to maintain a flat curvature. This then manifests as the unique "wavelengths" of each individual subatomic particle, and the quantum irregularity could be modeled as the multiple different "shortest paths" that space could generate itself into to attempt to grow closer to a flat surface, since depending on the hyperbolic curvature of spacetime being generated around the particle, you could potentially have more than one "parallel" line through the average point represented by the center of mass.   This seems consistent with observable matter. And it would imply that as more particles come closer together, the shortest paths would, over time,  become dependent on the average shortest path taken by the particles around them, since each particle is generating its own "wave" of spacetime around itself.   This effect would be MINISCULE with singular particles, advertised by the weak strength of gravity at those sizes, meaning that the particle essentially has a lot of "room" to play with. As it is so close to the size of a point, the curvature of spacetime being generated EXTREMELY close would be very random, since the closer to the center of mass, the greater the curvature, leading to instability. However, that instability would only be observable if the mass could travel a great enough distance across its LOCAL gravitational instability to cross a planck length, BEFORE it interacted with the generated spacetime of another particle.   This assumption regarding the Planck length would give a potentially experimentally testable generative origin for the speed of light.  The generation SPEED of space, would be the speed limit of the universe. Matter that travels at faster than the speed of light essentially "outruns" the speed that space is being generated by matter in this plane.  As such, any particle nearing that speed would be nearly free of any local effects from gravity, aka the generation of spacetime from its own mass, and that perhaps speed is representative of how closely the spacetime being generated by the particle aligns with the universal average or "plane". This perhaps would imply that energy/mass moves across spacetime by either generating it, or by somehow catalyzing that process as space itself attempts to return to a flat curvature. Regardless, you can almost imagine ANY particle that travels beyond its own gravitational "wake" in this manner would be forced to ONLY travel on pre-existing space time. Perfectly Flat space time.  The implication within the model being that the speed of light is defined by the maximum speed space is generated, instead of being traveled across.  And that matter EATS space in all the directions it DOES go.... just like a black hole would have to in the model. It's own gravitational devouring force so powerful that space literally CAN'T be generated fast enough for any "shortest" path to ever form across the Planck length and still make the mass generate spacetime "more" in line with the universe at large.  From here it gets unobservable and kind of nonsensical   The question to me, would then be "does spacetime simply cease to be generated beyond an event horizon. Devoured by the matter/energy, or does it simply fly out in some hyperbolic angle to the flat surface of the rest of the universe, after passing through matter/energy?"   and if it DOES simply move beyond our flat "plane" of spacetime, do black holes represent the points at which energy literally falls onto some other, random plane because it has bent spacetime SO MUCH that it cannot generate itself fast enough in this universe. Perhaps matter is recycled, accidentally, onto another angle of exit at which point the spacetime it generates around itself aligns with enough OTHER points of spacetime being generated by matter that they essentially catch each other into their own spacetime "orbit" and form another universe all their own.   This potential universe would be ruled by the speed of light as defined by the maximum speed of generation, which could potentially depend on the density of the black hole in question, although I can't quite articulate why that feels like the correlation to study in this case. Perhaps because more mass closer together in this model would generate simply MORE space, if energy does catalyze spacetime generation. Or is the generation itself.  If perhaps, matter and energy is defined as the shortest paths, the "path of least action", for spacetime to generate itself. And if that generation towards "flatness" affected other points of generation, then each type of particle could essentially be a microcosm of a universe. The "average" of the generated spacetime across the Planck length at the speed of light. The more divergent it is from the average "plane" of the universe, and the less dense, the faster it would move. The exact INVERSE of the scenario described in this model of a black hole.  In other words, matter/energy might literally be the generated space time that isn't "flat" with the rest of the generated universe, but isn't dense enough to generate enough spacetime of it's own to go anywhere but in the same flat plane as the rest of matter/energy because its average angle of generation is constantly being altered by the spacetime being generated relative to it by other mass moving in the same "plane".  This has probably already been mathematically explored almost certainly ends up contradicting itself or observed experimental results. It is a moment of wild thought, that had JUST enough of the spark of inspiration flickering in the back of it that I wanted to explore it.  Does anyone know if the curvature of space has been modeled by anyone as a PURELY generative hyperbolic action?

Black holes absorb all nearby matter and energy, while white holes theoretically do the opposite, ejecting matter. 02:26 *рЯМА EinsteinвАЩs Field Equations: A Gravitational Shift from Newton* - NewtonвАЩs gravity faced a conceptual gap that Einstein resolved by showing gravity as the result of spacetime curvature, with masses shaping their surroundings. - Light cones determine which regions of spacetime can affect or be affected by a point in space-time, defining reachable and observable areas. - Close to massive bodies, light cones narrow, signifying that objects cannot escape certain regions, hinting at black holes' event horizons. 06:22 *рЯМН Karl SchwarzschildвАЩs Solution and the Singularity Paradox* - Schwarzschild solved EinsteinвАЩs equations in 1915, discovering that near a black hole, spacetime curves steeply, leading to a вАЬsingularityвАЭ at r=0. - A second apparent singularity arises at the Schwarzschild radius, the event horizon, where escape velocity equals the speed of light. 08:22 *рЯТ• Black Holes and Star Collapse Theories* - Early theorists doubted black holesвАЩ existence, suggesting degeneracy pressures (electron and neutron) might prevent collapse, forming white dwarfs or neutron stars. - Chandrasekhar discovered a mass limit where even these pressures fail, suggesting massive stars could collapse into black holes. 11:55 *рЯМ† Event Horizons and the Spacetime Experience* - OppenheimerвАЩs insight showed that while external observers never see objects cross the event horizon, a person crossing it wouldnвАЩt notice anything unusual. - Spacetime diagrams demonstrate how light cones change near a black hole, tipping inward so that anything within the horizon must continue towards the singularity. 15:25 *рЯМН Distortions of Spacetime Projections* - Just as map projections distort EarthвАЩs geography, different spacetime maps alter how we view black hole dynamics. - Reframing the coordinates at the event horizon removes the apparent singularity, allowing objects to cross it smoothly. 16:56 *рЯХ≥пЄП The вАЬWaterfallвАЭ Model of Black Holes* - Space near a black hole behaves like a waterfall, where space flows inward; photons near the event horizon struggle against this flow but eventually fall in. - At the horizon, space itself falls faster than light speed, trapping everything inside. 20:19 *вМЫ The Kruskal-Szekeres and Penrose Diagrams* - The Kruskal-Szekeres diagram reinterprets the black hole singularity as a final moment in time rather than a spatial point, revealing spacetimeвАЩs temporal boundary. - Penrose diagrams compress spacetime, illustrating the inevitable progression toward the singularity and showing reachable and unreachable zones in spacetime. 22:15 *рЯММ White Holes: Black Hole Counterparts* - White holes, theorized counterparts of black holes, eject material instead of absorbing it, remaining inaccessible as nothing can enter them. - Unlike black holes, white holes exist in theory as time-reversed black holes, theoretically expelling matter and light. 23:46 *рЯММ White Holes as Time-Reversed Black Holes* - White holes, the theoretical inverse of black holes, expel matter instead of trapping it, as relativity allows time-reversed solutions to the equations, - If real, white holes would prevent entry but eject everything inside back into space. 24:44 *рЯМН Parallel Universes and Coordinate Systems* - Relativity equations allow alternate universes; parallel worlds might theoretically exist beyond the black hole's horizon, - SchwarzschildвАЩs model shows that another universe could be mapped mathematically, similar to discovering a вАЬsouthern hemisphereвАЭ on a previously unknown Earth. 27:09 *рЯМА Einstein-Rosen Bridges and Wormholes* - Extending solutions to EinsteinвАЩs equations reveals theoretical wormholes, or Einstein-Rosen bridges, that link separate universes, - This model, while hypothetical, suggests that black holes might connect different regions or universes in spacetime. 28:11 *рЯХ≥пЄП Challenges of Traversing Wormholes* - Wormholes, if real, are thought to be unstable, with the connection вАЬpinching offвАЭ before anything can cross, - Light cannot bridge these gaps, making travel between universes through a wormhole impossible with current understanding. 29:40 *рЯФД Rotating Black Holes and Ergosphere Dynamics* - KerrвАЩs solution for rotating black holes includes layers such as the ergosphere, where space is dragged by rotation, making escape challenging but possible, - A second inner horizon allows limited movement but would still lead toward a ring singularity, theoretically providing a way through. 31:45 *рЯМ† Entering New Realms in Rotating Black Holes* - Within a rotating black hole, explorers might navigate freely around the ring singularity and enter other universes or an вАЬanti-verseвАЭ with reversed gravity, - This remains theoretical; even if spacetime curves allow such transitions, verifying them remains beyond current science. 33:43 *вЪ†пЄП Limitations of Eternal Solutions for Black Holes* - Extended models of black holes and parallel universes assume static, eternal conditions not found in our universe, - Real black holes are formed through star collapse, making white holes improbable, as they would lack the necessary formation mechanism. 34:43 *рЯТ• Instability of Inner Horizons in Real Black Holes* - Real black holesвАЩ inner horizons may concentrate energy infinitely, creating singularities that block passage to any parallel universe, - This would limit any wormhole-like structures to theoretical speculation rather than practical reality. 35:13 *рЯЪА Theoretical Wormholes and Exotic Matter* - Wormholes suitable for stable travel require вАЬexotic matterвАЭ with negative energy, an idea not supported by current physics, - Without exotic matter, wormholes collapse, preventing them from connecting distant parts of the universe or separate universes. 36:17 *рЯММ Possibility of Surprises in Cosmology* - Split-complex numbers relate to the diagonality (like how it's expressed on Anakin's lightsaber) of ring/cylindrical singularities and to why the 6 corner/cusp singularities in dark matter must alternate. Dual numbers relate to Euler's Identity, where the thin mass is cancelling most of the attractive and repulsive forces.  The imaginary number is mass in stable particles of any conformation.  In Big Bounce physics, dual numbers relate to how the attractive and repulsive forces work together to turn the matter that we normally think of into dark matter. Complex numbers = vertical asymptote.  Split-complex numbers = vertical tangent.  Dual numbers = vertical line.  These algebras can be simply thought of as tensors.  Delanges sectrices can be thought of as opposites of vertical asymptotes.  Ceva sectrices as opposites of vertical tangents, and Maclaurin sectrices as opposites of vertical lines. The natural logarithm of the imaginary number is pi divided by 2 radians times i.  This means that, at whatever point of stable matter other than at a singularity, the attractive or repulsive force being emitted is perpendicular to the "plane" of mass. In Big Bounce physics, this corresponds to how particles "crystalize" into stacks where a central particle is greatly pressured to degenerate by another particle that is in front, another behind, another to the left, another to the right, another on top, and another below.  Dark matter is formed quickly afterwards.
